food_name: Krispy Big Box 60 Churros Party Set

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302050 represents churro as a food item. The Krispy Big Box 60 is a commercial party-set format (12 cups × 5 pieces = 60 churros with 12 dipping sauces); closest_parent used as no exact multi-serving party-box code exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers churros as a fried dough pastry category. The commercial party-box format is not separately coded; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept exists for churros or this commercial party-set format.

serving.description: 1 cup of 5 churros with 1 dipping sauce (approx. 80 g per cup serving)
serving.natural_language_portion: One realistic serving is one cup containing 5 warm churros with one accompanying dipping sauce, weighing approximately 80 g; the full Krispy Big Box 60 contains 12 such cups (60 churros total) and 12 sauces.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, plain (fried dough pastry) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 18617
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain churros (fried dough) used as the primary nutrient basis. Values reflect plain churros without dipping sauce. The dipping sauces (chocolate or caramel) would increase sugar, fat, and energy in the full serving; per-100g values here represent the churro component only, consistent with the dominant food mass. Adaptation noted: commercial Churros Republic product may vary slightly in oil absorption and sugar coating.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for commercial churros in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 74 is estimated by analogy to deep-fried wheat-flour dough products (e.g., doughnuts and similar fried pastries) reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which list fried wheat-dough products in the GI range 68–76. A midpoint of 74 is applied. Glycemic load per 80 g serving calculated as: (74 × 39.1 g available carbohydrate per 80 g serving) / 100 ≈ 29 g GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) and high energy density from deep-frying
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit churros consumption to occasional small portions, as the moderate sodium content (~248 mg per 80 g serving) and high caloric density from frying oil can contribute to excess energy and sodium intake when consumed in large party-box quantities.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~74) and high refined carbohydrate load from wheat-flour fried dough with sugar coating and sweet dipping sauces
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit churros, as the high GI (~74) and substantial glycemic load (~29 per serving) from refined wheat flour, sugar coating, and sweet dipping sauces will cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; the full 60-piece party box represents an extremely high cumulative glycemic burden.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (~19.8 g/100 g) including saturated fat (~4.1 g/100 g) and trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) from deep-frying, combined with high energy density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat churros as an occasional indulgence only, given the high total fat content from deep-frying, the presence of saturated and trans fats, and the very high caloric density (~387 kcal/100 g), all of which can adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly or in large quantities.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Krispy Big Box 60 is a large-format commercial churros party set sold by Churros Republic at Funan Mall in Singapore via field survey. The set contains 12 individual cups, each holding 5 pieces of warm, freshly fried churros, totalling 60 churros, accompanied by 12 individual dipping sauce cups. Churros are a traditional Spanish fried-dough pastry made from a simple choux-style dough of wheat flour, water, and salt, piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crisp on the outside with a soft, slightly chewy interior. They are typically dusted or rolled in sugar and cinnamon after frying. The dipping sauces offered by Churros Republic commonly include chocolate, caramel, and other sweet condiments. Nutritionally, churros are energy-dense (~387 kcal per 100 g) due to deep-frying, with a significant carbohydrate content (~48.9 g/100 g) from refined wheat flour and sugar coating, moderate fat (~19.8 g/100 g) from frying oil, and low protein (~4.5 g/100 g). The glycemic index is high (~74), making this food a high-glycemic-load dessert unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The Krispy Big Box 60 is designed as a sharing or party platter for groups, events, and celebrations, and is priced at SGD 67.00. It represents a Western-origin dessert adapted for the Singapore food-delivery market.
common_user_queries: How many churros are in the Krispy Big Box?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for plain churros (fried dough pastry), which is the closest approved-source match. The commercial Churros Republic product may differ in oil absorption, sugar coating thickness, and dipping sauce composition. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables (Atkinson et al. 2021) for fried wheat-dough products, as no direct GI measurement for churros exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent the churro component per 100 g (plain fried dough with sugar coating) and do not include the dipping sauces, which would increase energy, sugar, and fat in the full serving. 2. The food_name has been expanded from the menu label 'Krispy Big Box 60' to 'Krispy Big Box 60 Churros Party Set' for canonical clarity; the original menu name is retained as the primary alias. 3. Vitamin A is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. Vitamin B6 is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin B12 is -1 as churros contain no animal-derived ingredients in the base dough and the value is not reported. Folate is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 4. Cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; plain churros dough contains no egg or dairy in the base formulation). Vitamin C is 0.0 (confirmed zero; no vitamin C source in plain fried dough). 5. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate for fried wheat-flour dough products per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct churros GI measurement is available in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. This is a Spanish-cuisine commercial dessert sold in Singapore; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply as this is not a Singaporean-origin dish. Local names in Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are not applicable for this Spanish-origin food and are left null. 7. FoodOn URI FOODON_03302050 and FoodEx2 code A0FKP are applied as closest_parent matches for churros as a fried dough pastry category; no exact multi-serving party-box ontology code exists. 8. Halal status is listed as likely true based on Churros Republic's Singapore operations, but formal halal certification should be verified with the restaurant directly.
